title: Score Reconciliation, Waves, Safe Spawns, Pacing, and Difficulty Options · Traceback Repair Bay
summary: A preserved game trace contains one plausible state, timing, input, vector, rendering, collision, ownership, audio, accessibility, packaging, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in fair progression and difficulty model and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Derive score from immutable resolved events and reconcile totals by entity tier, streak, bonus, and run.
Advance only when active, queued, and pending-fragment counts satisfy a declared completion invariant.
Select and verify a respawn zone using predicted hazards, toroidal distance, grace time, fallback, and player communication.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Inspect the failure record · Debug Score Ledger
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Asteroids team handles case 5986 during a keyboard-only community arcade night. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile scoring, waves, spawn safety, pacing, difficulty options, and non-denigrating progression evidence; retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity; then lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Derive score from immutable resolved events and reconcile totals by entity tier, streak, bonus, and run.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Score Ledger, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Score Ledger record says fair progression and difficulty model looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect the failure record evidence boundary for Score Ledger.

For inspect the failure record, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof. This does not satisfy Score Ledger for fair progression and difficulty model.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Difficulty Access
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-difficulty-access-182hny" that uses case 6435 to reconcile scoring, waves, spawn safety, pacing, difficulty options, and non-denigrating progression evidence. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Difficulty Access.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Difficulty Access into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Difficulty Access, keep Difficulty Access connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-difficulty-access-182hny
# Competency: asteroids-difficulty-access.
# Case 6435: an offline cross-platform release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely.
def evidence_asteroids_difficulty_access_182hny(destroyed: int = 9, score_per_hit: int = 100, score: int = 900, wave: int = 3, safe_spawn: bool = True, difficulty_bounded: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_182hny = "182hny-ynh281-6435"
    assert evidence_variant_182hny
    evidence_axes_182hny = (
        "player182hny",
        "seed6435",
        "inputynh281",
        "frame182hny6435",
        "state6435182hny",
        "playtestynh2816435",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_182hny)) == 6
    if destroyed < 0 or score_per_hit < 0 or wave < 1:
        return False, score, "progression-domain"
    if score != destroyed * score_per_hit:
        return False, score, "score-drift"
    if not safe_spawn:
        return False, wave, "unsafe-spawn"
    if not difficulty_bounded:
        return False, wave, "difficulty-runaway"
    assert score == destroyed * score_per_hit
    return True, wave, "fair-progression"
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Difficulty Access structural check is only one gate. fair progression and difficulty model must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.
